scope: TileLang sparse-MLA compressed-dKV adjacent P/dP slab producer gate

input_contract.read: The kernel recomputes sparse attention probabilities and scaled dP once per token/selected tile, then writes one packed BF16 slab containing P and dP. It intentionally does not compute dKV; it tests whether an adjacent producer stage can replace pre-materialized P/dP slabs before the selected-limit dKV consumer.

results.read: Mean error is small, but the max BF16-packed dP diff is too large for promotion-quality numerical validation. Treat this run as a timing/contract gate until the exact outlier is localized.
Target-shape timing-only adjacent P/dP slab producer.

projected_adjacent_pipeline.read: Even if the generated BF16 slabs fed the existing selected-limit dKV consumer perfectly, the adjacent two-kernel path would be about 15.6 ms before launch/integration overhead. That is slower than the current sparse-atomic wall and too far from the old ~5 ms sparse-backward envelope.

decision.summary: The adjacent P/dP slab producer is concrete and much better than all-in-one dKV recompute, but it does not create a speed path. The S4096 store stage alone is 8.689 ms, and store plus existing selected-limit dKV is projected at 15.599 ms.
decision.next_kernel_contract: Do not build production as P/dP producer -> materialized slabs -> selected-limit dKV consumer. The next speed branch must consume P/dP on-chip in the selected-limit producer, or move to a HIP/CK/rocWMMA fused segmented producer that can keep row terms and dKV reduction in one schedule without storing full [S, topk, H] slabs.
